original_description: Apply the Intent-Driven Financial Architecture (IDFA) when building, auditing, retrofitting, or analysing Excel financial models. Activate when the user mentions: financial model, spreadsheet, Excel formula, named ranges, cell references, formula tracing, model audit, COGS, revenue projection, gross profit, EBITDA, DCF, LBO, comps, three-statement model, budget, forecast, variance analysis, what-if analysis, scenario modelling, goal seeking, Monte Carlo simulation, model review, or model handover. Also activate when the user says "the model is a black box", "I inherited this model", "I need to audit this spreadsheet", or any similar phrase indicating confusion about how a financial model works. Do NOT activate for general accounting questions, tax advice, investment recommendations, or tasks unrelated to the structure and logic of financial spreadsheets.
best_description: Apply the Intent-Driven Financial Architecture (IDFA) when building, auditing, retrofitting, or analysing Excel financial models. Activate when the user mentions: financial model, spreadsheet, Excel formula, named ranges, cell references, formula tracing, model audit, COGS, revenue projection, gross profit, EBITDA, DCF, LBO, comps, three-statement model, budget, forecast, variance analysis, what-if analysis, scenario modelling, goal seeking, Monte Carlo simulation, model review, or model handover. Also activate when the user says "the model is a black box", "I inherited this model", "I need to audit this spreadsheet", or any similar phrase indicating confusion about how a financial model works. Do NOT activate for general accounting questions, tax advice, investment recommendations, or tasks unrelated to the structure and logic of financial spreadsheets.
final_description: Use this skill for hands-on Excel financial model work: building models from scratch (SaaS, three-statement, revenue/COGS/EBITDA), auditing a spreadsheet for formula errors, explaining or mapping out model logic, converting cell references to named ranges, running what-if or scenario analysis, goal-seeking (what input value produces a target output?), or running Monte Carlo simulations. Also use when someone has inherited an unfamiliar model, calls it a "black box", or needs a specific formula (like WACC) checked. The trigger is a user who has a spreadsheet and needs to do something with it. Do NOT use for accounting theory, tax questions, investment advice, or finance questions with no model attached.

history.description: Apply the Intent-Driven Financial Architecture (IDFA) when building, auditing, retrofitting, or analysing Excel financial models. Activate when the user mentions: financial model, spreadsheet, Excel formula, named ranges, cell references, formula tracing, model audit, COGS, revenue projection, gross profit, EBITDA, DCF, LBO, comps, three-statement model, budget, forecast, variance analysis, what-if analysis, scenario modelling, goal seeking, Monte Carlo simulation, model review, or model handover. Also activate when the user says "the model is a black box", "I inherited this model", "I need to audit this spreadsheet", or any similar phrase indicating confusion about how a financial model works. Do NOT activate for general accounting questions, tax advice, investment recommendations, or tasks unrelated to the structure and logic of financial spreadsheets.

train_results.query: Explain the logic map of this financial model so I can hand it off to a new analyst
Find the Year 1 revenue needed to hit $3.5M EBITDA in Year 3 using goal seeking
My budget model is a black box — nobody knows what the formulas do. Help me map it out
Audit this spreadsheet for formula errors and tell me if the COGS calculations are correct
Can you check the WACC formula in my spreadsheet? I think there might be an error
I need to convert this legacy Excel model to use Named Ranges instead of cell references
I inherited this financial model from a colleague who left. Can you audit it and tell me what's going on?
Build me a 5-year SaaS revenue model with churn and expansion revenue
I need to do a what-if analysis on my three-statement model for the board presentation
I need to file a tax extension — what forms do I need?
Draft a marketing email campaign for our product launch
Create a React dashboard component for displaying sales metrics
Should I invest in NVIDIA or AMD stock right now?
Help me write unit tests for my Node.js API
Help me set up a PostgreSQL database for my application
What's the difference between FIFO and LIFO inventory accounting?
What's the current capital gains tax rate for long-term investments?
Should I use straight-line or declining balance depreciation for my equipment?

test_results.query: Add a depreciation line item to my P&L model and update EBITDA accordingly
Run a Monte Carlo simulation on my financial model with 100 iterations
What happens to EBITDA if I change the revenue growth assumption from 10% to 5%?
How do I record a journal entry for accounts receivable?
Write me a Python script to scrape stock prices from Yahoo Finance
What GAAP rules apply to revenue recognition for subscription businesses?

history.description: Use this skill for any task involving Excel financial models — building, auditing, fixing, or explaining them. Triggers include: a user who has inherited or is confused by a spreadsheet, wants to find what input achieves a target output (goal seeking), needs what-if or scenario analysis, wants to audit formulas for errors, needs to convert cell references to named ranges, wants to build a new revenue/cost/EBITDA model, or asks about WACC, DCF, Monte Carlo simulation, or any multi-year financial projection. The core use case is: the user has a spreadsheet and needs structured help working with its logic. Do NOT trigger for general accounting theory, tax questions, investment advice, or coding tasks that don't involve financial spreadsheets.

history.description: Use this skill whenever a user is working hands-on with a financial spreadsheet or Excel model — not asking abstract finance questions, but actually doing something with a model. This includes: building a new financial model (revenue, COGS, EBITDA, SaaS, three-statement); auditing or debugging formulas in an existing spreadsheet; explaining or mapping out what a model does; converting cell references to named ranges; running what-if or scenario analysis; finding the input value that produces a target output (goal seeking); or running Monte Carlo simulations on financial assumptions. The trigger is the user treating a spreadsheet as a working artifact they need help operating, not just asking about finance concepts. Do NOT use for accounting theory, tax questions, investment advice, or general finance questions that don't involve a specific model or spreadsheet.

history.description: Use this skill when the user wants to build, audit, fix, explain, or analyze an Excel financial model. This covers: building revenue, COGS, EBITDA, SaaS, or three-statement models; auditing spreadsheet formulas for errors; explaining what a model does or creating a logic map; converting cell references to named ranges; running what-if or scenario analysis; goal-seeking (finding the input that produces a target output); and Monte Carlo simulation on financial inputs. Also use when the user has inherited an unfamiliar model, says a model is a "black box," or needs to check a specific formula like WACC. The defining signal is that the user has a spreadsheet they need help operating — not an abstract finance question. Do NOT use for accounting theory, tax filing, investment advice, or finance questions with no spreadsheet involved.
